name: pyopenms
description: Python interface to OpenMS for mass spectrometry data analysis. Use for LC-MS/MS proteomics and metabolomics workflows including file handling (mzML, mzXML, mzTab, FASTA, pepXML, protXML, mzIdentML), signal processing, feature detection, peptide identification, and quantitative analysis. Apply when working with mass spectrometry data, analyzing proteomics experiments, or processing metabolomics datasets.
capabilities: Read and write mass spectrometry file formats including mzML, mzXML, mzTab, FASTA, and idXML
Apply signal processing algorithms for smoothing, peak picking, normalization, and baseline reduction
Detect features in LC-MS data and link them across samples for quantitative comparison
Perform peptide and protein identification with FDR filtering and protein inference
Execute complete untargeted metabolomics workflows including adduct detection and retention time alignment
Export data to pandas DataFrames for integration with statistical analysis tools
author: davila7
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown reference guides with Python code examples for PyOpenMS mass spectrometry analysis. No executable code, scripts, network operations, or file system access beyond what users already have. All static findings are false positives triggered by markdown documentation patterns.
valueStatement: Mass spectrometry generates complex data requiring specialized tools for proteomics and metabolomics analysis. PyOpenMS provides Python bindings enabling automated LC-MS/MS processing, peptide identification, and quantitative analysis with full reproducibility.
userTitle: Analyze mass spectrometry data with PyOpenMS
limitations: Requires pyopenms package installation with complex binary dependencies
Does not include search engine executables; Comet, Mascot, MSGFPlus must be installed separately
Memory usage scales with data size; very large files require indexed or streaming access patterns
Some TOPP tools are not directly accessible through the Python API

useCases.title: Process LC-MS/MS datasets
useCases.description: Automate proteomics data processing from raw spectra to protein quantification with reproducible Python scripts

useCases.title: Untargeted metabolomics analysis
useCases.description: Detect metabolite features, align retention times across samples, and create quantification tables for statistical analysis

useCases.title: Integrate MS data with pipelines
useCases.description: Combine PyOpenMS with pandas, scikit-learn, and other Python tools for custom mass spectrometry analysis workflows

promptTemplates.title: Load MS data
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to load an mzML file with PyOpenMS and access basic spectrum information like MS level, retention time, and peak count

promptTemplates.title: Process spectra
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a PyOpenMS script that applies Gaussian smoothing and peak picking to profile mass spectrometry data

promptTemplates.title: Detect features
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I detect features in centroided LC-MS data using the FeatureFinder and save results to featureXML format

promptTemplates.title: Filter by FDR
promptTemplates.prompt: Demonstrate how to load peptide identification results from idXML, apply 1% FDR filtering, and export filtered results

bestPractices: Use indexed or streaming access for large mzML files to avoid memory issues
Always preserve original data and create copies before processing
Validate file existence and handle exceptions for robust pipelines
antiPatterns: Loading entire very large mzML files into memory without using indexed access
Skipping quality control steps like blank filtering in metabolomics workflows
Applying search parameters without testing tolerance values on a data subset first

faq.question: What file formats does PyOpenMS support?
faq.answer: mzML, mzXML, mzData, idXML, mzIdentML, pepXML, protXML, featureXML, consensusXML, mzTab, FASTA, and TraML formats

faq.question: Can I use PyOpenMS on Windows?
faq.answer: Yes, but installation is more complex on Windows. Using conda or WSL is recommended for easier dependency management

faq.question: Does PyOpenMS include search engines?
faq.answer: No, PyOpenMS provides adapters but you must install search engines like Comet, Mascot, or MSGFPlus separately

faq.question: How do I handle very large files?
faq.answer: Use IndexedMzMLFileLoader for random access or MSExperimentConsumer for streaming processing to reduce memory usage

faq.question: Is my data safe with PyOpenMS?
faq.answer: PyOpenMS only processes data locally using the OpenMS library. No data is sent anywhere unless you explicitly code network calls

faq.question: How does PyOpenMS compare to other tools?
faq.answer: PyOpenMS offers Python-native access to comprehensive C++ algorithms, combining ease of Python with performance of compiled code
